companies.decision_maker_title: Chief Marketing Officer / VP Global Sponsorships

signals.description: AB InBev has entered exclusive negotiations with UEFA to become the official beer partner for all men's club competitions — Champions League, Europa League, Conference League — from 2027 to 2033 in a deal worth an estimated €1.2bn. This more than doubles Heineken's current contract and signals an enormous, active football sponsorship budget. LFC, as a perennial Champions League club, sits directly within AB InBev's strategic target for activation partnerships.
signals.news_headline: AB InBev set to pour into Champions League with €1.2bn beer sponsorship

companies.decision_maker_title: Chief Marketing Officer / Head of Global Partnerships

signals.description: Diageo has become the first spirits company ever to partner with FIFA, signing as Official Spirits Supporter for the FIFA World Cup 2026 across North, Central, and South America, activating Johnnie Walker, Don Julio, Buchanan's, Casamigos, and Smirnoff across all 16 host cities. This marks a step-change in Diageo's sports marketing ambition. As a UK-headquartered group already investing in global football, an LFC club partnership would extend that presence into European club football — a logical next step.
signals.news_headline: Diageo named Official Spirits Supporter in North, Central and South America for FIFA World Cup 26

companies.decision_maker_title: Chief Marketing Officer / VP Global Brand Marketing

signals.description: General Motors launched the Cadillac Formula 1 Team in March 2026 as the championship's 11th team, paying a $450m entry fee to gain access to a global premium audience. The F1 programme is explicitly designed to build Cadillac's European and international brand visibility — a market where GM has historically been weak. F1 gives GM reach into the same premium, global sports audience that LFC commands. A football partnership would directly complement their ongoing European brand-building push.
signals.news_headline: Cadillac Formula 1 Team Unveils Special Edition Livery for Barcelona Shakedown

companies.decision_maker_title: Group Chief Marketing Officer / Head of Global Sponsorships

signals.description: HSBC has returned to Formula 1 team sponsorship for the first time in 21 years, placing its logo on the Racing Bulls car, while simultaneously becoming the title sponsor of F1 coverage on Channel 4 in the UK. This dual investment is the clearest signal in two decades that HSBC is re-engaging with premium global sports as a brand-building tool. Their international customer base — spanning Asia, Europe, and the Americas — maps almost identically to Liverpool FC's global fanbase, making an LFC partnership a natural companion to the F1 play.
signals.news_headline: HSBC returns to F1 after 21 years

companies.decision_maker_title: Chief Commercial Officer / Global Head of Sponsorships

signals.description: Heineken has confirmed it will end its 30-year UEFA Champions League sponsorship in August 2027, citing a strategic shift toward higher-ROI partnerships. Critically, the budget is not disappearing — Heineken is actively redirecting it: retaining Formula 1, signing a global Premier Padel deal, and renewing UEFA Women's Champions League. This creates a rare, direct window. Heineken has an open football sponsorship slot from 2027 and is in the market for premium club partnerships right now. LFC should be an immediate priority outreach.
signals.news_headline: Heineken to end UEFA Champions League sponsorship in 2027 amid strategic marketing shift
